justification: All 3 ACs are present with exact values (AC1: $4.50, AC2: $5.00, AC3: $0.01). Quantity boundary >= 10 is preserved exactly. $0.01 floor is labeled as a domain rule/business rule in both domain_rules and terminology_alignment. High-traffic context (~500 req/sec) and unit test requirement are included from Pippen's operational notes. However, JSDoc requirement is completely absent from the brief — it is never mentioned anywhere. The rubric explicitly lists 'JSDoc absent' as a fail condition, and the expected behavior states Pippen's JSDoc note about documenting the $0.01 floor must be passed to Shaq as an explicit requirement. This omission means a key operational requirement will not reach the implementer.

observations.text: All 3 ACs preserved with exact given/when/then values including the hard edge case AC3
Quantity boundary >= 10 preserved exactly in both domain_rules and terminology_alignment
$0.01 floor clearly labeled as a business/domain rule, not buried in architecture
High-traffic context (500 req/sec) and stateless constraint carried through from Pippen and MJ
Thoughtful open_questions section identifies real ambiguities (floating-point rounding, test file location)
terminology_alignment section bridges domain and technical language effectively
JSDoc requirement is entirely absent — Pippen's note that JSDoc must document the $0.01 floor is lost
No explicit attribution of which inputs came from which source (Bird, MJ, Pippen), making it harder to trace provenance
Unit tests mentioned in task_context but not called out as a dedicated section or explicit deliverable requirement
